note: Digest built with web research; parallel-cli was not available on this machine (no pip/pipx/WSL). Install via https://parallel.ai/install.sh or pipx install "parallel-web-tools[cli]" then re-run: parallel-cli search "..." -o mountain-parallax-research.json
objective: Mountain silhouette design references and parallax layer speed guidance for 2D game backgrounds
queries: jagged mountain range silhouette vector peaks valleys game background
parallax scrolling layer speed distant near 2D game background multiplier

summary.silhouettes: Stock and game vectors emphasize jagged peaks, cols between summits, and often separate layers for endless parallax scrolling.
summary.parallax: Distant layers use lower scroll multipliers than near layers; Godot docs give example scales (sky 0.1 through forest 0.7) to sell depth.

results.title: Jagged mountain range silhouette Vector Image
results.excerpt: Royalty-free vector jagged mountain range silhouette—typical reference for sharp peaks and ridgeline profile used in backgrounds and UI.

results.title: Mountain silhouette peaks landscape (Vecteezy)
results.excerpt: Transparent-background mountain silhouette with multiple peaks; suitable for layering in game scenes.

results.title: Seamless mountain landscape — separated layers (Everypixel)
results.excerpt: Vector nature background described as seamless with separated layers for 2D game design and endless scrolling.

results.title: 2D Parallax — Godot Engine documentation
results.excerpt: Parallax2D scroll_scale: 1 = camera speed; below 1 = slower (farther). Example stack: sky 0.1, clouds 0.2–0.3, hills 0.5, forest 0.7.

results.title: 2D Game Background Parallax Multiple Layers (Stack Overflow)
results.excerpt: Discussion of positioning and speed relationships between sequential parallax layers (e.g. aligning layer widths with speed ratios).

results.title: Parallax Scrolling: Add Depth to a 2D Game (Envato Tuts+)
results.excerpt: Tutorial framing: layers at different speeds relative to camera to simulate depth in 2D.

results.title: How to Create a Parallax Background in Phaser (Josh Morony)
results.excerpt: Practical implementation of multi-layer parallax scrolling in a 2D web game framework.

results.title: Jagged Mountain Range Vectors (Freepik)
results.excerpt: Collection of jagged mountain range vector assets including hand-drawn and flat silhouettes.

project_alignment.ParallaxBackground.jsx: Far mountain layer scroll 0.03, mid 0.15 (Slayer Idle) — wide ratio so distant vs mid ranges separate clearly.
project_alignment.ParallaxMountainSilhouettes.jsx: Far: broad soft massifs + haze; mid: jagged polygon ridgeline with cols; seamless 960px tile doubled to 1920.
